nav.logout_confirm: Are you sure you want to log out of your account?

modal.funds_received_msg: You have received {amount}, please check the ledger balance.
modal.general_error: An error occurred. Please try again later.

dashboard.coach_account: This is a coach account for training purposes.

overview.subtitle: Manage your profile, security preferences, and payout configurations.
overview.passphrase_desc: This passphrase acts as your private financial key, required to authorize payouts in the Manage Payouts portal. To configure this key initially, you must provide your registered account email.
overview.passphrase_update_desc: Update your private financial key. This key is securely required to authorize all of your standard withdrawal requests.
overview.passphrase_length: Passphrase must be at least 6 characters.
overview.email_mismatch: Email does not match our records.

referrals.title: Referral Program
referrals.subtitle: The referral program is a completely optional feature. Your daily assignments, attendance, and primary payouts will not be affected whether you participate or not.
referrals.id_desc: Copy your unique referral ID below. Share it with someone who could benefit from our company, and start earning a lifetime bonus on their generated incomes.
referrals.overview_p1: Instead of traditional advertising, we choose to reinvest our budget directly back into our community. When an eligible user (the "Referrer") successfully refers a new user (the "Referee"), the Referrer will earn a 20% lifetime bonus based on the profits generated by the Referee.
referrals.overview_l1: This 20% bonus is subsidized entirely by the Company.
referrals.overview_l2: The Referee retains 100% of their original earnings.
referrals.overview_l3: All earned referral bonuses are credited directly to the Referrer’s ledger balance.
referrals.mlm_desc: Our referral structure is strictly one-to-one (1-on-1). We do not support multi-level marketing (MLM) or tiered commission structures. The Referrer is entitled to bonuses solely from the direct Referee they successfully referred. If the Referee subsequently refers a third party, the original Referrer earns no commission from that third-party user.
referrals.paused_desc: Referral bonuses are active only when the Referee's account is in good standing. If a Referee leaves a assignment in a "pending" state, your 20% bonus from their account will be paused. Any bonuses that would have been generated during this paused state are forfeited and will not be released retroactively. Once the Referee clears their pending assignment, your 20% bonus will immediately resume for all of their future completions.
referrals.eligibility_l1: Attendance Requirement: To protect the company integrity, a Referrer must have accumulated a total of 14 days of attendance since account creation before they are authorized to generate or share a referral ID.
referrals.eligibility_l2: Cooldown Period: To ensure high-quality referrals, links may only be shared with one individual at a time. Upon successfully linking a new Referee, the Referrer is placed on a 14-day cooldown period before being permitted to refer another user.
referrals.eligibility_l3: Fraud & Account Bans: Program abuse, including creating multiple accounts for self-referral or utilizing fraudulent methods, is strictly prohibited. Violations will result in the immediate forfeiture of all bonus earnings and a permanent ban of all associated accounts.

rewards.welcome_desc: As a new member, a $20.00 Welcome Bonus has been automatically credited to your available balance upon registration. This bonus is provided to give you a head start on your earnings and is added directly to your standard balance.
rewards.salary_desc: Complete daily assignments to reach your upcoming payout milestones.
rewards.perfect_desc:
Consistency pays off. Complete all {days} daily assignments within the current month to achieve the Full Attendance KPI. This automatically applies a +15% Base Payout Multiplier to your earnings for the cycle.

How it works: The multiplier is applied exclusively to your accumulated base payouts. For example, if your total base payouts amount to $1,200, the +15% multiplier will add an extra $180, bringing your final base payout total to $1,380. (Note: Other earnings are calculated separately and are not affected by this multiplier).
rewards.promo_desc: Bonus events are special, temporary opportunities that occasionally occur. When active, they appear here for a limited duration to give you a chance to earn exclusive rewards. Check back periodically before they expire!
rewards.no_promos: No active events at this time.

assignments.title: Active Schema Allocations
assignments.subtitle: Automated Data Processing & Financial Ledger
assignments.ledger_desc: Core operational funds, inclusive of profits.
assignments.outstanding_desc: The funding shortfall required to settle the pending assignment.
assignments.current_balance_desc: Total ledger balance projected with the pending active assignment.
assignments.cycle_profit_desc: This calculation updates at the start of each new cycle. All profits are then added to the total ledger balance.
assignments.sync_desc: Establish a secure connection to the allocation pool to sync and inspect the next available structured dataset prior to ledger execution.
assignments.idle: Data view idle. Please sync a structural schema.
assignments.pending_schema_error: Please upload the pending schema before continuing to the next.

payouts.title: Manage Payouts
payouts.fill_required: Please fill out all required fields to request a payout.
payouts.action_denied_quota: You must complete all active assignments (Quota: {current} / {max} remaining) before requesting a payout.
payouts.exceeds_balance: Requested amount exceeds your available ledger balance.
payouts.request_success: Payout request for {amount} submitted successfully. It is now pending approval.
payouts.network_error: Network error occurred while requesting payout.

contact.title: Get in Touch
contact.subtitle: We'd love to hear from you.
contact.name: Name
contact.modal_msg: Thank you for reaching out. We have received your message.
contact.error_send: We couldn't send your message. Please try again later.

hero.title:
Zero-config
for structured data. Instantly map your schemas.
hero.subtitle: A mission control for semantic data with real-time schema validation and entity insights.

meta.title: The Structured Data Company | The Schema Infrastructure Platform
meta.description: The unified platform for structured data and schema management—bringing clarity and semantic meaning to your frontend.

tracking.title: Schema Integrity
tracking.desc: Visualize real-time data validation and schema health through a clear, intuitive view. Spot drift quickly and stay informed with live structural insights.

processing.title: Semantic Mapping
processing.desc: Keep your data schemas synchronized in real time across all global instances. Ensure consistent types, smooth integration, and confident data delivery everywhere.

unifying.title: The Schema Registry
unifying.desc: Centralize schemas, JSON-LD fragments, and data models in one unified dashboard. Reduce fragmentation and manage every entity that matters from a single place.

security.main_title: Built-in data security. Included for everyone.
security.main_subtitle: Our semantic firewall delivers system-wide protection, safeguarding your data structures by default.

firewall.title: Data Firewall
firewall.subtitle: Resilient protection at the edge
firewall.desc: Our global edge network absorbs volumetric attacks instantly, ensuring your data origin remains invisible and untouched by malicious traffic.

waf.title: Semantic Guard
waf.subtitle: Protects data traffic from threats
waf.desc: Intelligent filtering analyzes incoming packets in real-time, blocking injection and malformed entities before they reach your data store.

workspace.title: Access Control
workspace.subtitle: Trusted schema access for teams
workspace.desc: Zero-trust architecture ensures only authenticated users and compliant devices can modify your internal tools and data resources.

code.title: Simplicity first, always.
code.desc: Intuitive dashboards, visual schema builders, and comprehensive guides to fit right into your existing workflow without writing a single line of code.

stats.languages: Supported schema types and semantic vocabularies

fluid.title: Self-healing schema operations.
fluid.desc: Automate the metadata. AI-powered intelligence pro-actively suggests and applies the right schema types, property labels, and relationship mappings.

footer.sf_address:
C/O Chalk Hill Barn Office 1 Upstairs

England, TN25 6DH
footer.mission: We provide the semantic infrastructure to define, scale, and secure a structured web, making data universally understandable.

terms.title: Legal

tos.title: Terms of Service
tos.content:
PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING TERMS OF SERVICE OF THE PORTAL CAREFULLY BEFORE JOINING THE COMPANY OR USING THE COMPANY SERVICES. THESE TERMS OF SERVICE CONSTITUTE A LEGAL AGREEMENT BETWEEN YOU AND THE COMPANY AND ARE IN ADDITION TO THE TERMS OF THE AGREEMENT (DEFINED BELOW). IF YOU DO NOT OR CANNOT ACCEPT THESE TERMS OF SERVICE, YOU ARE NOT PERMITTED TO JOIN THE COMPANY. IF YOU ARE ACCESSING THIS ADDENDUM ELECTRONICALLY, SIGNIFY YOUR AGREEMENT TO BE BOUND BY THE TERMS OF THIS ADDENDUM BY CLICKING THE "CREATE ACCOUNT" BUTTON. IF YOU DO NOT AGREE TO THESE TERMS OF SERVICE, DO NOT CREATE ACCOUNT.

These Terms of Service (the "Terms") govern your access to and use of our website and mobile application (collectively, the "Portal"), and associated content, features, software and applications in the Portal (collectively, the "Service"). The Portal and the Service is administered and maintained by The Structured Data Company. The terms "we", "us", "our" and "Company" also refer to The Structured Data Company. The term "you" refers to the person visiting the Portal.

Please read these Terms carefully. They contain important information regarding your legal rights, including limitations on the Company's and certain third parties' liability, disclaimers of warranties and a submission to jurisdiction. Each time you access the Service by any means (for example, through the Internet or on a mobile device), you irrevocably agree to comply with the version of the Terms posted on the Portal at the time you access the Portal. You may not use the Portal or the Service if you do not agree to these Terms. By accessing or using the service, you agree to be bound by these terms.

ELIGIBILITY
To be eligible to use any of the Company Services, you must be at least 23 years old and reside in either United Kindgom, United States, Canada, Europe, Japan, South Korea Singapore, Spain, Hong Kong, or Thailand. There are certain features which may or may not be available to you depending on your location and other eligibility criteria. You must not use any software or networking techniques, including use of a Virtual Private Network (VPN) to modify your internet protocol address or otherwise circumvent or attempt to circumvent this prohibition.

CHANGES
We may modify these terms of service, our privacy policy, and our cookie policy from time to time. If we make material changes to it, we will provide you with notice through our services or by other means to provide you with the opportunity to review the changes before they become effective. We agree that changes cannot be retroactive. If you object to any changes, you may close your account. Your continued use of our services after we publish or send a notice about our changes to these terms means that you are consenting to the updated terms as of their effective date.

AVAILABILITY, ERRORS AND INACCURACIES
Access to Services may become degraded or unavailable during times of significant volatility or volume. This could result in limitations on access to your Account or the Services, including the inability to initiate or complete transactions and may also lead to support response time delays.

a. We do not guarantee that the Site or other Services will be available without interruption and we do not guarantee that any order will be executed, accepted, recorded, or remain open or that your Account will be accessible; and

b. Please note that our support response times may be delayed, including during times of significant volatility or volume, especially for non-trust and safety issues.
Although we intend to provide accurate and timely information on the Site, the Site (including, without limitation, the Content (as defined below)) may not always be entirely accurate, complete or current and may also include technical inaccuracies or typographical errors. In an effort to continue to provide you with as complete and accurate information as possible, information may, to the extent permitted by applicable law, be changed or updated from time to time without notice, including without limitation information regarding our policies, products and services. Accordingly, you should verify all information before relying on it, and all decisions based on information contained on the Site are your sole responsibility and we shall have no liability for such decisions.

We are constantly updating our offerings of products and services on the service. The products or services available on our service may be mispriced, described inaccurately, or unavailable, and we may experience delays in updating information on the service and in our advertising on other websites. We cannot and do not guarantee the accuracy or completeness of any information, including product images, specifications, availability, and services. We reserve the right to change or update information and to correct errors, inaccuracies, or omissions at any time without prior notice. In case of unforeseen events, the Company is entitled to suspend the services for maintenance or repair purposes if this is necessary to ensure the proper operation of the services.

ACCOUNT
You would need to have an account with Us ("Account") in order to use some parts of the Service. When you create an Account, you represent and warrant that you are at least of legal age in your respective jurisdiction, you are capable of entering into and performing legally binding contracts under applicable law, and that all information which you provide is accurate, up-to-date, truthful and complete. You are solely responsible for all activities under your Account. We will not be responsible in any way if your password and/or Account are misappropriated or used by a third party. You therefore agree to keep your password secure and keep your account information up-to-date at all times.

Unless expressly permitted by Us and subject to the Terms and any other additional terms as the Company solely and absolutely determines, you shall not set up multiple Accounts. You shall not lend, transfer or sell your Account or Account information to another party and must not use another user's Account without their written permission.

We may occasionally contact you on the email address provided in your Account registration. You will not be able to opt out from such communications, and you shall take the responsibility to ensure that your email address is up-to-date. If you missed any communications due to an inaccurate, outdated, or incomplete email address, We will not be liable for any losses or damages caused by you missing the communication.

You may not close or delete your account while there are any pending operations. The Company reserves the right to deactivate an user's account in case of fraud activity that a user accomplished during the existence of the Company.

Actions that can be regarded as fraud activity:
a. Creating different accounts under the same identity;
b. Creating different accounts in order to use the Company bonuses several times;
c. Fraud from stolen credit cards or fraud from other payment methods;
d. Any behaviour that has signs of fraud activity and risks to the service.

PROHIBITED ACTIVITIES
You agree that you will not:

a. Copy, modify or create derivative works of the Service or any Content;

b. Copy, manipulate or aggregate any Content (including data) for the purpose of making it available to any third party;

c. Trade, sell, rent, loan, lease or license any Content or access to the Service, whether commercially or free of charge;

d. Use or introduce to the Service any data mining, crawling, "scraping", robot or similar automated or data gathering or extraction method, or manually access, acquire, monitor or copy any portion of the Service, or download or store Content (unless expressly authorized by Us);

e. Make excessive requests for information or take any action that interferes with, disrupts or imposes an undue burden on the Service or any server or network connected to the Service or negatively affects the quality or availability of any Content, or speed or functionality of the Service;

f. Introduce a virus, Trojan horse, worm, time bomb or other malware to the Service or the Platform, or use any device, software or routine to bypass any software or hardware that prohibits volume requests for information;

g. Violate, bypass or circumvent any security measure intended to limit or prevent access to the Platform, Content or Service; or

h. Otherwise attempt to gain unauthorized access to the Service, any Content or to any computer systems or networks connected to the Service or any Company server, whether through hacking, password mining, unauthorized use of another's password/credentials or any other means;

i. Restrict, inhibit or interfere with use of the Service by any other user (including by hacking or defacing the Portal);

j. Introduce or otherwise distribute through the Portal any computer program that damages, interferes with, intercepts, collects, releases or discloses any system, data or personal information of ours or any third party;

k. Make use of any of our trademarks, service marks, trade names or logos or those of any third party displayed on the Platform;

l. Modify or remove any copyright or other proprietary notice in the Content;

m. Use the Website, Content or Service for or in connection with any activity that (i) violates any applicable law, statute, ordinance or regulation, including without limitation, the Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC), or (ii) involves proceeds of any unlawful or illegal activity.

Additionally, you acknowledge and agree that you (and not Us) are solely responsible for obtaining and maintaining all telecommunications, broadband, and computer hardware, equipment, software and services needed for you to access and use the Service, and paying all charges related thereto.

LIMITATIONS OF LIABILITY
The Services, the Portal and the Site are provided on an "as is" and "as available" basis, with no further promises made by us around availability of the Services. Specifically, we do not give any implied warranties of title, merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose and/or non-infringement. We do not make any promises that access to the Site, any of the Services, or any of the materials contained therein, will be continuous, uninterrupted, timely, or error-free.

If you have a dispute with one or more users of the Company, you agree that neither we nor our affiliates or service providers, nor any of our respective officers, directors, merchants, agents, joint venturers, employees and representatives, will be liable for any claims, demands and damages (actual and consequential, direct or indirect) of any kind or nature arising out of or in any way connected with such disputes.

We are not liable for any breach of the Agreement, including delays, failure in performance or interruption of service, where they arise directly or indirectly from abnormal and unforeseeable circumstances beyond our control, the consequences of which would have been unavoidable despite all effects to the contrary, nor are we liable where the breach is due to the application of mandatory legal rules.

DISCLAIMER OF NO WARRANTY
The Company and its affiliates make no representation or warranty about the services, including any representation that the services will be uninterrupted or error-free, and provide the services (including content and information) on an "as is" and "as available" basis. To the fullest extent permitted under applicable law, the Company and its affiliates disclaim any implied or statutory warranty, including any implied warranty of title, accuracy of data, non-infringement, or merchantability of fitness for a particular purpose.

TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, COMPANY ASSUMES NO LIABILITY OR RESPONSIBILITY FOR ANY (I) ERRORS, MISTAKES, OR INACCURACIES OF ANY CONTENT; (II) PROPERTY DAMAGE, OF ANY NATURE WHATSOEVER, RESULTING FROM YOUR ACCESS TO OR USE OF THE SERVICE; (III) ANY UNAUTHORIZED ACCESS TO OR USE OF OUR SERVERS AND/OR ANY AND ALL INFORMATION STORED THEREIN; (IV) ANY INTERRUPTION OR CESSATION OF TRANSMISSION TO OR FROM THE SERVICE; (V) ANY BUGS, VIRUSES, TROJAN HORSES, OR THE LIKE THAT MAY BE TRANSMITTED TO OR THROUGH THE SERVICE BY ANY THIRD PARTY; (VI) ANY ERRORS OR OMISSIONS IN ANY CONTENT OR FOR ANY LOSS OR DAMAGE INCURRED AS A RESULT OF THE USE OF ANY CONTENT POSTED, TRANSMITTED, OR OTHERWISE MADE AVAILABLE THROUGH THE SERVICE; AND/OR (VII) THE DEFAMATORY, OFFENSIVE, OR ILLEGAL CONDUCT OF ANY THIRD PARTY.

ENGAGEMENT AND COMPLIANCE
To assess users' comprehension of and engagement with our products and services, as well as to gauge their readiness for future innovations, it is required that users complete all product cycles before initiating a withdrawal request. Withdrawals cannot be processed mid-cycle, nor can any product interactions be canceled, skipped, or reversed, due to binding agreements with our merchant partners. Our system experiences frequent updates, with products being added and removed every minute, and prolonged holding of a product by a user can adversely affect merchant progress. Users are encouraged to complete their product cycles and submit withdrawal requests within a timeframe of 24 to 48 hours. In the event that a product is retained for longer than the specified 48-hour period, our terms of service provide for adjustments to the standard payment schedules based on mutual agreement between the user and our company. Should such adjustments occur, nominal fees may be levied to cover the administrative costs associated with these changes and to ensure continuous service provision. Details regarding these fees are included within the billing adjustments section of our user agreements, promoting transparency and aiding comprehension. This approach allows us to flexibly respond to our users’ varying financial situations while safeguarding the efficiency and progress of our merchant partners. Additionally, the Company may request further documentation to verify the authenticity of the funds involved. Users may also be asked to demonstrate the origin of these funds. Should there be any concerns regarding the legitimacy of the funds, the Company reserves the right to conduct an audit of the user's account. This comprehensive approach helps ensure that all financial interactions within our platform adhere to the highest standards of security and compliance. To maintain compliance and ensure clarity in transactional flows, each account shall operates with distinct payer information, except in the case of coach accounts.

SUSPENSION, TERMINATION, AND CANCELLATION
We may: (a) suspend, restrict, or terminate your access to any or all of the Company Services, and/or (c) deactivate or cancel your Account with immediate effect for good reason, including, but not limited to where:

a. We are required by a governmental authority to do so;
b. You are, or appear to be, unable to meet your obligations in respect of one or more duties;
c. You are subject to an insolvency event of any type (as determined in our absolute discretion);
d. Any investigation, claim, action or proceeding of any nature is commenced against you (including without limitation investigation into suspected market abuse, manipulation or other criminal conduct), you have breached Applicable Laws or steps are taken by any person to enforce any security interest against you;
e. You are in breach of this Agreement; or
f. We otherwise feel it is necessary in order to protect us or the Company.
If we suspend, restrict or close your Account, and / or terminate your use of Services, we will (unless it would be unlawful for us to do so) provide you with notice of our actions and the reasons for refusal, suspension or closure, and where appropriate, with the procedure for correcting any factual errors that led to the refusal, suspension or closure of your Account.
We may terminate or suspend or cancel your account immediately, without prior notice or liability, for any reason whatsoever, including without limitation, if you breach the Terms. Upon termination, your right to use the service will immediately cease.

OWNERSHIP AND INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
a. Subject to these Terms, you may view, print and make copies of Content for your own personal use. You shall not, copy, reproduce, download, "screen scrape", store, transmit, broadcast, publish, modify, create a derivative work from, display, perform, distribute, redistribute, sell, license, rent, lease or otherwise use, transfer (either in printed, electronic or other format) or exploit any Content, in whole or in part, in any way that does not comply with these Terms.

b. As between the Company and you, all Content is owned or controlled by the Company. Company, its logo, and, except as noted below, all other product or service names or slogans displayed on the Service are registered and/or common law trademarks of Company or its suppliers or licensors and may not be copied, imitated, or used, in whole or in part, without the prior written permission of Company or the applicable trademark holder. In addition, the look and feel of the Service, including all page headers, custom graphics, button icons, and scripts, is the service mark, trademark, and/or trade dress of Company and may not be copied, imitated, or used, in whole or in part, without the prior written permission of Company. All other trademarks, registered trademarks, product names, and company names or logos mentioned in the Service are the property of their respective owners. Reference to any products, services, processes, or other information, by trade name, trademark, manufacturer, supplier, or otherwise does not constitute or imply endorsement, sponsorship, or recommendation thereof by Company.

c. Nothing contained in these Terms grants you any interest in any of Company's or any third-party's intellectual property.

d. All related logos, trademarks, service marks and trade names are solely the property of Company. The absence of a name, logo or other mark herein does not constitute a waiver of any and all intellectual property rights that Company has established. Other trademarks, names or logos used on the Website are property of their respective owners. You are not authorized to use any of the foregoing.

e. You acknowledge that Company and/or its providers own the copyright in and to all Content under the applicable laws (including copyright laws and other intellectual property laws), and have reserved all rights in and to such Content.

LINKS TO OTHER WEBSITES
Our service may contain links to third-party web sites or services that are not owned or controlled by the Company. The Company has no control over, and assumes no responsibility for, the content, privacy policies, or practices of any third-party websites or services. You further acknowledge and agree that the Company shall not be responsible or liable, directly, or indirectly, for any damage or loss caused or alleged to be caused by or in connection with the use of or reliance on any such content, goods, or services available on or through any such websites or services. We strongly advise you to read the terms and conditions, and privacy policies of any third-party web sites or services that you visit.

YOU AGREE THAT COMPANY WILL NOT, UNDER ANY CIRCUMSTANCES, BE RESPONSIBLE OR LIABLE, DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY, FOR ANY GOODS, SERVICES, INFORMATION, RESOURCES AND/OR CONTENT AVAILABLE ON OR THROUGH ANY THIRD-PARTY WEBSITE OR SERVICES, FOR ANY DEALINGS OR COMMUNICATIONS YOU MAY HAVE WITH THIRD PARTIES, OR FOR ANY HARM, DAMAGES OR LOSS CAUSED OR ALLEGED TO BE CAUSED BY OR IN CONNECTION WITH ANY OF THE FOREGOING OR YOUR USE OF OR RELIANCE ON THE MATERIALS OR THE CONTENT OR BUSINESS PRACTICES OF ANY THIRD PARTY.

POLICY ON DISHONEST ACT
To detect, deter and prevent dishonest act, the Company follows these principles:

a. Allocate law enforcement, financial intelligence and supervisory resources on a risk-sensitive basis;
b. Maintain close policy and operational coordination and cooperation across the Government;
c. Take a preventive approach that combines tough licensing and comprehensive reporting requirements, strict Anti-Dishonest Act regulations, and risk-based supervision of the relevant financial and non-financial sectors;
d. Enhance private sector stakeholders' understanding of dishonest act risks and promote a culture of compliance;
e. Take decisive and deterrent law enforcement action against dishonest activity, including that relating to foreign crimes;
f. Provide assistance to other jurisdictions through formal and informal channels, spontaneously and on request.

CONFIDENTIAL POLICY WITH COOPERATED MERCHANTS
A. Confidential and Proprietary Information
In the course of employment, employees will be exposed to confidential and proprietary information of the employer. Confidential and proprietary information shall mean any data or information that is competitively sensitive material and not generally known to the public, including, but not limited to, information relating to development and plans, marketing strategies, finance, operations, systems, proprietary concepts, documentation, reports, data, specifications, computer software, source code, object code, flow charts, data, databases, inventions, know-how, trade secrets, customer lists, customer relationships, customer profiles, supplier lists, supplier relationships, supplier profiles, pricing, sales estimates, business plans and internal performance results relating to the past, present or future business activities, technical information, design, process, procedure, formula, or improvement, which Employer considers confidential and proprietary. Employee acknowledges and agrees that the confidential and proprietary information is valuable property of the employer, developed over a long period of time at substantial expense, and that it is worthy of protection.

B. Confidentiality Obligations
Except as otherwise expressly permitted in this Agreement, Employee shall not disclose or use in any manner, directly or indirectly, any confidential and proprietary information either during the term of this Agreement or at any time thereafter, except as required to perform their duties and responsibilities or with the employer's prior written consent.

C. Rights in Confidential and Proprietary Information
All ideas, concepts, work products, information, written material, or other confidential and proprietary information disclosed to the employee by the employer (i) are and shall remain the sole and exclusive property of the employer, and (ii) are disclosed or permitted to be acquired by the employee solely in reliance on the employee's agreement to maintain them in confidence and not to use or disclose them to any other person except in furtherance of the employer's business. Except as expressly provided herein, this agreement does not confer any right, license, ownership, or other interest or title in, to, or under confidential and proprietary information on the employee.

D. Irreparable Harm
The employee acknowledges that use or disclosure of any confidential and proprietary information in a manner inconsistent with this agreement will give rise to irreparable injury for which damages would not be an adequate remedy. Accordingly, in addition to any other legal remedies that may be available at law or in equity, the employer shall be entitled to equitable or injunctive relief against the unauthorized use or disclosure of confidential and proprietary information. The employer shall be entitled to pursue any other legally permissible remedy available as a result of such breach, including but not limited to damages, both direct and consequential. In any action brought by the employer under this section, the employer shall be entitled to recover its attorney's fees and costs from the employee.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.content:
At The Structured Data Company, we’re strongly committed to protecting and respecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy (“Privacy Policy”) covers your access and use of this website (the “Website”), as well as associated content, software, and mobile applications (collectively, the “Service”). This Privacy Policy explains when, why and how we collect and use personal information about people who visit our Website or use our Services, the conditions under which we may disclose your personal information to others, and how we keep your personal information secure. We may change this Privacy Policy from time to time. By using our Website or our Services, you are agreeing to be bound by this Privacy Policy. Please read this Policy carefully. We are committed to protecting your privacy through our compliance with the terms of this Policy.

CHANGES
The Company ("we" or "us") can modify this privacy policy, and if we make material changes to it, we will provide notice through our services, or by other means, to provide you the opportunity to review the changes before they become effective. If you object to any changes, you may close your account. You acknowledge that your continued use of our services after we publish or send a notice about our changes to this privacy policy means that the collection, use, and sharing of your personal data is subject to the updated privacy policy as of its effective date.

CREATE ACCOUNT
To create an account, you need to provide data, including your email address and/or mobile number, and a self-setup password. When collecting personal information, we collect it within the necessary limits, and the collection will be carried out with a priority to ensure legality and fairness. We may collect your personal information from you directly or from other entities with whom we work, such as merchants, users, government authorities, public or government agencies, or our own the Company Personal Information we collect, use and disclose includes:

a. Contact information, such as your name, email address and contact number;

b. Identity information such as your nationality;

c. Information required under applicable laws and regulations, professional obligations, internal policies and risk management procedures, such as record-keeping obligations, fraud prevention, audit purposes, compliance screening and due diligence, sanctions regime and court order, whether in any eligible Regions or elsewhere;

d. Information relating to info-communication technology systems and processes, office equipment and facilities; and

e. Photographs and other audio-visual information.
If you are submitting the personal information of another individual to us, you confirm that it is true and correct, that all necessary consents have been obtained, or that you are otherwise permitted to provide such personal information to us.

PROTECTION OF YOUR PERSONAL INFORMATION
We use appropriate technical, organizational, and administrative security measures to protect your personal information from any loss, misuse, or unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, or destruction. We also limit access to your personal information to those who have a genuine business need to know. Personal information is being put under appropriate administration with required and reasonable protective measures under way to guard against unfair access, loss, falsification, leakage, etc. from the perspective of proper information technology and administration. However, there are always risks involved with providing personal information, as no technology is completely risk-free or can be guaranteed to be 100% secure. If any negligence in administration were to occur, such as leakage of such data, we would be in a position to take immediate and effective countermeasures as far as possible.

RETENTION OF YOUR PERSONAL INFORMATION
We will not hold your personal information for any period longer than we believe is reasonably necessary for the purposes it was collected for, or for such a longer period as is required for business or legal purposes. When destroying your personal information, we will take commercially reasonable measures to make your personal information irrecoverable or irreproducible in accordance with the applicable law.

HOW WE USE YOUR PERSONAL INFORMATION
How we use your personal information will depend on which services you use, how you use those services, and the choices you make in your settings. We use the information that we have about you to provide and personalize our services, including with the help of automated systems and inferences we make, so that our services (including ads) can be more relevant and useful to you and others.

COOKIE AND SIMILAR TECHNOLOGIES
As further described in our Cookie Policy, we use cookies and similar technologies (e.g., pixels and ad tags) to collect data (e.g., device IDs) to recognize you and your device(s) on, off, and across different services and devices where you have engaged with our services. We also allow some others to use cookies as described in our Cookie Policy.

SECURITY
We implement security safeguards designed to protect your data, such as HTTPS. We regularly monitor our systems for possible vulnerabilities and attacks. However, we cannot warrant the security of any information that you send us. There is no guarantee that data may not be accessed, disclosed, altered, or destroyed by breach of any of our physical, technical, or managerial safeguards.

DO NOT TRACK SIGNALS
We currently do not share personal data with third parties for their direct marketing purposes without your permission.

REVIEW OF PRIVACY POLICY
This portal will not be publicly acknowledged and will be kept strictly confidential in order to prevent leaks of merchants' confidential information.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.content:
At the Company, we believe in being clear and open about how we collect and use data related to you. This cookie policy applies to any the Company product or service that links to this policy or incorporates it by reference. We use cookies and similar technologies such as pixels, local storage, and mobile ad IDs (collectively referred to in this policy as cookies") to collect and use data as part of our services, as defined in our Privacy Policy ("Services"), which includes our sites, communications, mobile applications, and off-site services, such as our ad services and the "Apply with the Company" and "Share with the Company" plugins or tags. In the spirit of transparency, this policy provides detailed information about how and when we use these technologies.
By continuing to visit or use our services, you are agreeing to the use of cookies and similar technologies for the purposes described in this policy.

WHAT TECHNOLOGIES ARE USED

COOKIES
Cookies are small text files containing a string of characters that can be placed on your computer or mobile device, which uniquely identify your browser or device. We may use technologies such as cookies, pixels, and local storage to deliver, secure, and understand products, services, and ads. We use two types of cookies: persistent cookies and session cookies. A persistent cookie lasts beyond the current session and is used for many purposes, such as recognizing you as an existing member, so it's easier to return to the Company and interact with our services without signing in again. Since a persistent cookie stays in your browser, it will be read by the Company when you return to one of our sites or visit a third party site that uses our services. Session cookies last only as long as the session (usually the current visit to a website or a browser session).

PIXELS
A pixel is a tiny image that may be embedded within web pages and emails, requiring a call (which provides device and visit information) to our servers in order for the pixel to be rendered in those web pages and emails. We use pixels to learn more about your interactions with email content or web content, such as whether you interacted with ads or posts. Pixels can also enable us and third parties to place cookies on your browser.

LOCAL STORAGE
Local storage enables a website or application to store information locally on your device(s). Local storage may be used to improve the Company experience, for example, by enabling features, remembering your preferences, and speeding up site functionality.

WHAT ARE THESE TECHNOLOGIES USED FOR AUTHENTICATION
We use cookies and similar technologies to recognize you when you visit our services. If you're signed into the Company, these technologies help us show you the right information and personalize your experience in line with your settings. For example, cookies enable the Company to identify you and verify your account.

SECURITY
We use cookies and similar technologies to make your interactions with our services faster and more secure. For example, we use cookies to enable and support our security features, keep your account safe, and help us detect malicious activity and violations of our User Agreement.

PREFERENCES, FEATURES AND SERVICES
We use cookies and similar technologies to enable the functionality of our services, such as helping you fill out forms on our services more easily and providing you with features, insights, and customized content in conjunction with our plugins. We also use these technologies to remember information about your browser and your preferences.

BROWSER CONTROLS
Most browsers allow you to control cookies through their settings, which may be adapted to reflect your consent to the use of cookies. Further, most browsers also enable you to review and erase cookies, including the Company cookies. To learn more about browser controls, please consult the documentation that your browser manufacturer provides.

HOW CAN YOU CONTROL COOKIES?
To learn more about the choices that advertisers provide generally for individuals to influence how information about their online activities over time and across third-party websites or online services is collected and used, visit the Network Advertising Initiative at http://www.networkadvertising.org/managing/opt_out.asp, the Digital Advertising Alliance at http://www.aboutads.info/, or the European Digital Advertising Alliance at http://youronlinechoices.eu/. In addition, your browser or device may offer settings that allow you to choose whether browser cookies are set and to delete them. These controls vary by browser and manufacturers may change both the settings they make available and how they work at any time. As of 23 June 2021, you may find additional information about the controls offered by popular browsers at the links below. Certain parts of the Company products may not work properly if you have disabled browser cookie use.

registration.title: Legal Notice and Registration
registration.content:
1. Parties
The parties to these General Conditions of Use are the Company, and you as a user, understood as any individual or corporate entity that freely, voluntarily, and without payment accesses the Portal regardless of whether or not you make use of the services offered there. The simple fact of visiting the Portal means that you subject yourself without reservations to these General Conditions of Use.

2. Services
We provides Portal to disseminate its activity and so that Internet users have specific information on the services it offers, related to the Search Engine or the positioning of products in the market.

3. Obligations of the user
As a user, you agree:-

To make diligent, correct, and legal use of the Service, respecting current law (particularly that relating to data protection and intellectual and industrial property), morals and decency, public order, and these General Conditions of Use.

To review periodically these General Conditions of Use or any other applicable conditions, checking for changes that may have been made to any of them.

Check the notifications we send to you, as they may include important information.

Not use the Portal for commercial purposes; for example, by collecting information or content to provide other services that may represent obvious competition for the Portal.

Not to modify or try to modify the Portal in any way, or to act or use means designed to simulate the appearance or functions of the Portal.

Not to carry out any action that involves the introduction of computer viruses, worms, Trojans, or any other kind of malicious code designed to interrupt, destroy, or limit the functionalities of the Portal.

Not to use reverse engineering techniques and/or decipher, decompile, or use any other system designed to discover the source code of the Portal or of any other element subject to copyright or underlying intellectual property rights.

Not to damage, disable, overload, or impede the service (or a network or networks connected to the service), or interfere with its enjoyment.

In any case, do not carry out any actions that may infringe the rights or interests of the Portal or third parties, such as, for example, intellectual property or industrial rights (patents, brand names, copyrights, commercial secrets, etc.).

4. Intellectual and industrial property
The intellectual property rights to the Company and to the elements of which it is composed, including the programming, design, applications, graphics, codes, text, or images therein, belong exclusively to the Company, or the Company has the necessary rights and/or authorizations for their use. The name of the domain, the brands, commercial names, and in general any distinctive mark in the Company, are also the property of the Company, and/or the Company has the necessary licenses to use them. As a result of the above, any reproduction, distribution, public communication (including making it available), transformation, or any other form of use, even if the sources are specified, is prohibited, without the express agreement in writing from the Company or the exclusive owner of the rights affected. If you detect any infringement of the intellectual and/or industrial property rights in the Company, please report it as soon as possible by contacting the support.

5. Links to third sites
The Portal may include links to pages or websites of third parties not related to the Portal. The Portal does not assume any responsibility for these links or the contents that can be accessed through them, as it does not approve or review their functions, advertising, or in general the information included in these pages, and disclaims any liability for their content and their correct operation, or any consequences or damage that may occur as a result of accessing them. Users who access any of these links do so at their own risk, accepting the external nature of these contents and the inability of BRANDVIOUS, INC. to guarantee that there are no threats, malware or viruses, illegal content, or other links that in turn lead to sites with one or more of said characteristics.

6. Exclusion of Liability
The Portal works to ensure that the services and functionalities of the Portal are always available. However, on access to the Portal, it will be shown "as-is", according to the availability and limitations in place at any time. Despite the Company's continuous efforts to protect the systems and content included in the Portal, for which purpose it uses the common security standards on the Internet, it is not possible to offer full guarantees about the intrusions or loss of information that may take place. Similarly, there is no guarantee of the absence of viruses or other harmful elements in the Portal or in third-party websites that may produce software and hardware changes in the user's computer system. The user therefore assumes and understands that there are situations that may be beyond the control of the Company. The Company does not accept any liability that may derive from the misuse of the Portal by the user, or for the breach of obligations or commitments assumed under these General Conditions of Use, or any other conditions that may be applicable. In general, neither the Company nor its collaborators may be held liable in case of loss of earnings or actual loss for any reason.

7. Indemnity
The Company suffers any type of damage, losses, or costs (including lawyers' and court representatives' fees) as a result of a breach by the users of these General Conditions of Use, or any other conditions applicable, the user shall be obliged to compensate the Company. This will also be case if as a result of a breach by the user claims are made by third parties against the Company, in which case the third party shall hold the Company harmless, and the Company may claim against the user any expenses, costs or damages derived from its actions.

8. Data protection and security risks
The collection of personal data through the Company and its treatment by the Company is regulated by a specific Privacy Policy to which the candidates may have access by viewing.

9. General matters
9.1. Safeguard and interpretation
These General Conditions of Use constitute an agreement between you as user and the Portal. If the competent authority declares any provision to be illegal, invalid or not enforceable, this provision must be interpreted in the way that is closest to its original intent. Such a declaration with respect to one or more of the clauses shall not affect the validity of the remaining ones. If the Company does not comply strictly with one of the terms of these Conditions, it does not constitute, nor in any case may it be interpreted to be, a waiver of a possible demand for compliance in the future.

9.2. Legislation and jurisdiction
Relations between the Portal and the user shall be governed by the United States law, and in case of disagreement with respect to the interpretation of or compliance with these General Conditions of Use, they expressly renounce any other jurisdiction that may correspond to them, and subject themselves to the courts, unless any other jurisdiction is determined by imperative law.

CERTIFICATE
Registered Entity Name: The Structured Data Company Limited
Registered Address: C/O Chalk Hill Barn Office 1 Upstairs, Yew Tree Farm, Stone Street, Stanford, Kent, England, TN25 6DH
Company Number: 16095340

success.signup_msg: Your account has been created successfully.

items.question: Do I need coding experience to use the platform?
items.answer: No coding experience is required. We have designed the platform with intuitive dashboards and visual schema builders that allow you to manage, synchronize, and upload complex data structures without writing a single line of code. Our goal is to make advanced data processing accessible to everyday users.

items.question: How does the AI entity detection work?
items.answer: Our proprietary AI-powered intelligence engine automatically analyzes incoming raw data properties and proactively suggests the correct schema types, relationship mappings, and property labels. This significantly reduces the need for manual data entry, minimizes human error, and speeds up your ability to complete daily assignments.

items.question: Is there a mobile app available?
items.answer: While we do not have a standalone downloadable app, our platform is fully web-based and highly optimized for both desktop and mobile browsers. We chose a web-first approach to ensure that critical security patches and system updates are deployed to all users instantly, allowing you to seamlessly manage your dashboard from any device with a stable internet connection.

items.question: What exactly is a "Schema" in the context of this platform?
items.answer: A schema is a structured framework that defines how raw, unstructured data is organized, connected, and understood by machines. Our merchant partners require perfectly structured data to train their models and organize their catalogs. Our platform processes these semantic schemas to ensure data integrity before they are validated and uploaded to the financial ledger.

items.question: Do you offer a public API for developers?
items.answer: Currently, our platform operates entirely through our visual web dashboard and we do not offer a public API for consumer accounts. Dedicated API integration is strictly restricted to our approved institutional and merchant partners to maintain the integrity of our allocation pool and prevent unauthorized external scraping.

items.question: Can I use automated bots or scripts to complete my assignments?
items.answer: No. The use of automated bots, scripts, macros, or any other unauthorized software to complete assignments is strictly prohibited. Our merchants pay for authentic human oversight during the validation process. Engaging in automated activities will immediately trigger our anti-fraud systems, resulting in a permanent account ban and the forfeiture of all generated earnings.

items.question: Do you offer webhooks for real-time ledger balance notifications?
items.answer: No, we currently do not offer webhooks or public API endpoints for real-time ledger notifications. To ensure maximum security of your financial data, we utilize a closed-loop system. You must log into your authenticated dashboard directly to monitor your balance, upcoming milestones, and recent transactional activity.

items.question: Who is eligible to use the platform?
items.answer: To comply with strict international financial regulations and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) policies, users must be at least 23 years of age. Additionally, you must reside in one of our supported operational regions, which currently include the United Kingdom, United States, Canada, Europe, Japan, South Korea, Singapore, Spain, Hong Kong, or Thailand.

items.question: Are there any upfront fees to join?
items.answer: No, creating an account is completely free and there are absolutely no hidden sign-up charges. Our platform generates revenue through the successful processing of schema data for our merchant partners, not by charging our users an entry fee.

items.question: What is a Referrer Account ID and why do I need one?
items.answer: A Referrer ID is a unique code used during the sign-up process. It is required to ensure you are accurately placed within our network and matched with the correct senior coach. This ID establishes a direct mentorship link so you have immediate support from an experienced user from day one.

items.question: Can I create multiple accounts?
items.answer: No, setting up multiple accounts is strictly prohibited unless expressly permitted in writing by our compliance team. To maintain fair quota distribution across our network and adhere to AML regulations, users cannot create different accounts under the same identity or use aliases to bypass daily limits.

items.question: Can I delete my account?
items.answer: Yes, you have the right to close your account at any time. However, you may not close or delete your account while there are any pending operations, active cycles, or outstanding shortfalls. All financial ledgers must be fully settled and cleared before an account deletion request can be successfully processed.

items.question: Is my personal data and platform activity encrypted?
items.answer: Yes. Strict data privacy is our absolute priority. All sensitive user data, including personal identifiers, financial ledgers, and payout credentials, are secured using military-grade AES-256 encryption both in transit and at rest. Furthermore, our architecture includes a Data Firewall for edge protection and a Semantic Guard to proactively filter network threats.

items.question: Is the platform compliant with GDPR and CCPA data privacy laws?
items.answer: Yes. We are strictly compliant with major international data privacy regulations, including the GDPR (Europe) and CCPA (California). We only collect the minimal data necessary for AML/KYC legal compliance and platform operations. We guarantee that we will never sell your personal or financial information to third-party data brokers.

items.question: Who owns the intellectual property of the structural schemas I process?
items.answer: All intellectual property rights for the structural schemas, processed data sets, and resulting ledgers remain the exclusive, confidential property of the Company and our merchant partners. Users are generously compensated for their processing and validation efforts, but they do not retain any ownership, copyright, or licensing rights over the data itself.

items.question: Can I export my processed schema history?
items.answer: No. Due to strict Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs) and confidentiality contracts with our merchant partners, the data you process is considered highly proprietary. To prevent corporate espionage or data leaks, users are technically restricted from exporting, downloading, or scraping their schema processing history.

items.question: What is a Payout Passphrase and why is it required?
items.answer: Your Payout Passphrase acts as your private financial key. It is a mandatory, secondary layer of authorization required to approve any standard payout request. This ensures that even in the highly unlikely event that your main account password is compromised, malicious actors cannot transfer your hard-earned ledger funds without this secondary key.

items.question: I forgot my password, how do I recover my account?
items.answer: If you have lost access to your account, you must contact our support team through the secure portal. Because we deal with financial ledgers, you will be required to verify your identity (often via KYC document submission) before we can issue a manual reset. This strict process prevents social engineering and unauthorized account takeovers.

items.question: What exactly are the active "Assignments"?
items.answer: Assignments are the core work you perform on the platform, consisting of Automated Data Processing and Financial Ledger schema allocations. When you start an assignment, you establish a secure connection to our allocation pool to synchronize a structural schema, verify the dataset for accuracy, and upload it to the ledger. This process validates merchant data and generates your asset yield.

items.question: What is required to start or reset a cycle?
items.answer: To ensure users have sufficient liquidity to process high-value schemas, every cycle requires a minimum ledger balance to initiate. For brand new accounts, the earliest cycle starts at an $80 minimum. For all subsequent cycles, the minimum required balance is $150. You cannot trigger a cycle reset while you are currently in the middle of an active cycle.

items.question: Can I skip, remove, or reverse an active quota assignment?
items.answer: No. Once an assignment is synchronized to your dashboard, it is strictly bound to your current cycle. Assignments cannot be skipped, removed, reversed, or bypassed under any circumstances. You must complete the validation process to advance to the next quota.

items.question: What happens if I hold a product schema for too long?
items.answer: If an assignment is synced but retained without being uploaded for longer than 48 hours, it clogs the allocation pool and actively delays our merchant partners' progress. In these instances, nominal fees may be levied from your ledger to cover administrative costs, and standard payment schedules may be adjusted due to the breach of operational speed.

items.question: Why do I sometimes receive corrupted files?
items.answer: Occasionally, the raw data provided by merchants contains unreadable or broken fragments, resulting in a corrupted file. These files are not eligible to generate any yield. However, because the system still had to allocate server resources to fetch and assign the data to you, it will consume one of your 40 available quotas for that cycle.

items.question: What are your standard operating hours?
items.answer: Our primary support and processing operations, including manual payout reviews and KYC audits, run from Monday to Friday, 10:00 AM to 10:00 PM EST. This aligns with traditional global banking hours. Weekend availability (Saturday and Sunday) may fluctuate based on scheduled system maintenance and active queue volumes.

items.question: How do I contact the customer support team?
items.answer: For the fastest and most secure resolution, you can reach our dedicated support team directly through the live chat widget located on your dashboard. This ensures you are communicating from within an authenticated environment, allowing our agents to pull up your account details immediately.

items.question: What is the role of my assigned coach?
items.answer: Your assigned coach is a highly experienced, senior user of the platform. Their role is to provide you with one-on-one guidance, help you understand the nuances of schema operations, and teach you how to optimize your daily assignments. Because of our referral program, they are financially incentivized to see you succeed.

items.question: How is my coach assigned?
items.answer: Your coach is automatically linked to your account during the registration process based entirely on the Referrer Account ID you provided. This direct assignment ensures you have a dedicated point of contact who is already familiar with your onboarding timeline.

items.question: Is my coach responsible for my account's financial performance or shortfalls?
items.answer: No. While your coach is there to provide training, operational guidance, and strategic advice, your account is strictly an individual profile. You maintain full autonomy and responsibility over your own daily assignments, ledger balance, and any potential shortfalls. Your coach does not have access to your personal funds and cannot perform assignments on your behalf.

items.question: What is a Shortfall and how do I resolve it?
items.answer: A shortfall occurs when you are assigned a premium, high-yield schema that exceeds the current value of your ledger tier. To process this high-value assignment and generate the associated premium yield, you must temporarily bridge the gap. To resolve a shortfall, contact our live support team to receive instructions on depositing the exact required funds. Deposits can be securely made via traditional fiat channels or supported blockchain networks.

items.question: How long do deposits take to reflect in my account?
items.answer: Deposit times depend heavily on the blockchain network congestion or bank processing speeds. However, once you have successfully completed the transfer and provided the transaction receipt to our live support team, the funds will typically reflect in your total ledger balance within 15 to 30 minutes following standard network verification.

items.question: Can I deposit funds to skip my daily quotas?
items.answer: No. The core function of our business is actual data processing, not pure capital investment. Deposits are strictly utilized for starting/resetting a new cycle or clearing an active premium shortfall. You cannot pay money to bypass the mandatory manual validation work.

items.question: How is the Asset Yield calculated?
items.answer: Asset yield is calculated dynamically based on the complexity of the schema and current merchant market demand. For each standard assignment you successfully complete and upload, the yield rate percentage ranges randomly between 1% and 6% of the asset's core value.

items.question: What is a cycle and how many quotas does it have?
items.answer: One complete product cycle consists of 40 quotas (individual data assignments). We enforce a strict limit of 2 full cycles per single day. This limitation prevents user burnout, maintains high data validation accuracy, and ensures a fair distribution of work across our entire global network.

items.question: What happens if I don't finish all 40 quotas in one sitting?
items.answer: You are absolutely not required to complete a full 40-quota cycle in a single sitting. You can pause your work and resume at your convenience. However, remember the 48-hour rule: you must not hold an active, synced assignment for more than 48 hours to ensure merchant processing pipelines are not delayed.

items.question: Do my earnings compound automatically?
items.answer: No, your earnings do not mathematically compound cycle-over-cycle. Each new cycle resets the base metrics to maintain predictable costs for our merchants. However, all generated yield from previous cycles will safely remain accumulating in your total ledger balance until you successfully request a final payout.

items.question: How do I claim the Welcome Bonus?
items.answer: To help you cover your initial cycle thresholds faster, a $20.00 Welcome Bonus is automatically credited to your available ledger balance immediately upon successful registration. There are no extra steps, hidden forms, or waiting periods required to claim it.

items.question: How does the Attendance Milestone Tracker work?
items.answer: We highly value consistent data processors. By completing your daily assignments every single day, you build an uninterrupted attendance streak. As you hit specific milestones (such as Day 7, Day 14, etc.), the system automatically unlocks scheduled base payout bonuses that are credited directly to your balance.

items.question: What is the Full Attendance KPI?
items.answer: If you achieve perfect attendance by completing all daily assignments without missing a single day within the current month, you unlock the Full Attendance KPI. This applies a massive, automatic multiplier to your total accumulated base payouts for that cycle, rewarding our most reliable users. (Note: This multiplier applies to base payouts only, not to other varying yields).

items.question: What happens if I miss a day of assignments?
items.answer: Because our merchants rely on consistent daily volume, failing to complete your quota for the day will break your active attendance streak. This means you will miss out on the Full Attendance KPI multiplier for that month and will need to restart your progress toward the next milestone from day one.

items.question: What are Limited-Time Bonus Events?
items.answer: During periods of unusually high merchant demand, we run special promotional events that offer exclusive, increased rewards. These events appear dynamically on your dashboard for a limited duration. You should check the Promotions section regularly before they expire so you don't miss out on peak earning windows!

items.question: Do I earn a commission for referring new users?
items.answer: Yes! We prefer to reinvest our advertising budget back into our community. If you participate in our optional referral program, you earn a 20% lifetime bonus based on the profits generated by your direct referral. This 20% is subsidized entirely out of the Company's pockets, meaning your referee still keeps 100% of their own earnings.

items.question: Is this a multi-level marketing (MLM) structure?
items.answer: No, absolutely not. Our referral structure is strictly one-to-one (1-on-1). You only earn bonuses from the direct user you referred and trained. We do not support multi-level marketing, pyramids, or tiered commission structures because we want to encourage genuine, direct mentorship.

items.question: Who is eligible to participate in the referral program?
items.answer: To ensure our referrers actually understand the platform before they attempt to teach others, you must accumulate a total of 14 days of active attendance before you are authorized to generate a referral ID. To maintain high-quality mentorship, there is also a mandatory 14-day cooldown period between each successful referral you make.

items.question: Why is my referral bonus paused?
items.answer: Referral bonuses are active only when your Referee's account is in good standing. If your referee gets stuck and leaves an assignment in a "pending" state, your 20% bonus from their account will be temporarily paused. This rule exists to motivate coaches to actively help their referees clear hurdles. Once they upload the pending assignment, your bonus resumes immediately.

items.question: Are there specific conditions before I can request a payout?
items.answer: Yes. To maintain strict ledger stability and ensure merchant daily quotas are fulfilled before capital exits the ecosystem, you must complete all active assignments within your current cycle. You must also ensure there are no outstanding balances or shortfalls on your account. Payouts strictly cannot be processed mid-cycle.

items.question: Is there a minimum payout amount?
items.answer: Yes, the minimum payout amount is $20. This minimum is enforced because the platform absorbs the cost of network gas fees and administrative banking overhead for your transactions, which is financially unviable for micro-transactions under $20.

items.question: Are there transaction volume limits for standard payouts?
items.answer: To maintain ledger stability and network liquidity, payout capacities are aligned with your active operational tier. Standard Basic accounts can securely process payouts of up to $5,000 per transaction. Users who regularly process premium schemas and operate on a Plus tier are granted unrestricted payout ceilings to accommodate their higher volume. Your current transaction limits can be reviewed at any time in your dashboard settings.

items.question: Are there any payout fees?
items.answer: We do not charge any internal payout fees or percentages on your balances. Furthermore, we cover the crypto network gas fees for tokenized payouts to maximize your take-home earnings. However, if you choose traditional fiat banking methods, you are responsible for any international transfer rates applied by your own financial institution.

items.question: How long do payouts typically take?
items.answer: You should submit your payout request within 24 to 48 hours after completing a cycle. Once submitted, payouts often process within an hour, though final settlement times are subject to your receiving financial institution or blockchain network speed. You may also inform our live support team after submitting your request to facilitate processing at the earliest convenience. All requests remain subject to mandatory Anti-Money Laundering (AML) checks and yield verification.

items.question: What payout methods are supported by the platform?
items.answer: We support a globally diversified range of settlement methods tailored to your registered region to ensure maximum flexibility. Options include direct fiat Bank Transfers (such as ACH, SEPA, BACS, and Interac) as well as Tokenized Assets (Cryptocurrency via manual network configuration).

items.question: What happens if I direct a payout to the wrong crypto wallet address or network?
items.answer: Due to the decentralized nature of blockchain technology, cryptocurrency transactions are strictly irreversible. If you send a payout to an incorrect wallet address or select the wrong network, your funds will be permanently lost. We have no technical ability to recover sent tokens, so please double-check your destination details carefully.

items.question: Do I need a non-custodial wallet to receive tokenized payouts?
items.answer: No, a non-custodial wallet is not strictly required. You can use any wallet that supports the specific token and network you select on the payout page. This includes private non-custodial wallets as well as direct deposit addresses from major centralized exchanges.

items.question: Can I request a payout to a bank account under someone else's name?
items.answer: No, under no circumstances. To comply with strict international Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Know Your Customer (KYC) regulations, the legal name on your destination bank account must perfectly match the verified identity on your platform account. Any attempted third-party transfers will trigger a security flag and be automatically rejected.

items.question: Why was my bank transfer rejected or delayed?
items.answer: Fiat banking systems are often slower and more rigid than crypto networks. Bank transfers are typically delayed due to user-entry errors (incorrect routing/account numbers), regional banking holidays, or routine security holds placed by your receiving financial institution. If a transfer hard-fails, the funds will be safely returned to your ledger balance.

items.question: Do you support international wire transfers?
items.answer: While we technically support traditional international wire transfers for certain remote regions, please note that this method is frequently unavailable or subject to extended maintenance due to high failure rates. When it is available, you are entirely responsible for covering any incoming international wire fees charged by intermediary banks.

items.question: What should I do if my dashboard freezes during an active assignment?
items.answer: Don't panic. If your dashboard freezes, your browser crashes, or you lose internet connection, simply refresh the page or log back in. Your progress, including your active schema and current ledger balance, is saved securely on our cloud servers in real-time, allowing you to resume exactly where you left off.

items.question: Will I be compensated if a server outage causes me to miss a cycle?
items.answer: No. While we invest heavily in our infrastructure to strive for absolute maximum uptime, we do not provide financial compensation for missed cycles, broken attendance streaks, or expired quotas resulting from temporary, unforeseen server outages or scheduled system maintenance windows.
